blurb: Paul's ministry flows from the new covenant of the Spirit, not the old covenant of death carved in stone by the Demiurge. Where the Creator's law brought condemnation through its impossible demands, the Father's Spirit brings life through grace that transforms from within. The glory of Moses' system was real but fading, bound to disappoint; the glory of Christ's covenant increases eternally because it rests on the Father's unchanging mercy rather than human performance. We serve not as administrators of legal requirements but as ministers of the Spirit who gives life freely to all who believe.

verses.text: Who also hath made us able ministers of the new testament; not of the letter, but of the spirit: for the letter killeth, but the spirit giveth life.

But if the ministration of death, written and engraven in stones, was glorious, so that the children of Israel could not stedfastly behold the face of Moses for the glory of his countenance; which glory was to be done away:
How shall not the ministration of the spirit be rather glorious?
For if the ministration of condemnation be glory, much more doth the ministration of righteousness exceed in glory.
